question_text: A student has three objects: Object A (mass 500g, volume 250cm³), Object B (mass 800g, volume 400cm³), and Object C (mass 300g, volume 600cm³). Water has a density of 1g/cm³. Which object would float in water, and explain your reasoning using density calculations.
correct_answer: Object C would float. Object A has density 500÷250 = 2g/cm³, Object B has density 800÷400 = 2g/cm³, and Object C has density 300÷600 = 0.5g/cm³. Since Object C's density (0.5g/cm³) is less than water's density (1g/cm³), it will float, while the other two objects sink.
explanation: To determine if an object floats or sinks, you must calculate its density and compare it to water's density of 1g/cm³. Objects with lower density float, while objects with higher density sink. This question requires three separate density calculations and a comparison between results.
